bash_commands.command: ls /workspace/ && ls /workspace/node 2>/dev/null || echo "no node dir"
git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/nodejs/node.git 2>&1 | tail -5
grep -rl "Object\.create(null)" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | sort
grep -rl "hasOwnProperty\|ObjectHasOwn\|ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | sort
grep -rl "Object\.create(null)" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | sort -u
grep -rl "ObjectCreate\b" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | sort

# Search for primordials usage in lib/ - ObjectCreate is the primordial version of Object.create(null)
# Let's see what primordials are available
grep -n "ObjectCreate\|ObjectAssign\|ObjectDefineProperty" /workspace/node/lib/internal/primordials.js 2>/dev/null | head -30
# Also check if primordials.js exists
ls /workspace/node/lib/internal/primordials.js 2>/dev/null || echo "not found"
find /workspace/node/lib -name "primordials*" 2>/dev/null
ls /workspace/node/lib/internal/ | head -40
grep -n "ObjectCreate\|ObjectAssign\|ObjectDefineProperty\|ObjectHasOwn\|ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty" /workspace/node/lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js | head -40

# Find all files using primordials (which is the main defense mechanism)
grep -rl "const.*primordials\|require.*primordials" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | wc -l
grep -n "ObjectCreate" /workspace/node/lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js | head -20

# Search more broadly - how does Node.js use 'use primordials' or similar
grep -rl "'use primordials'" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | wc -l

# Let's look at how primordials.js is structured
head -100 /workspace/node/lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js

# Find key primordials-related to prototype pollution defense
grep -n "ObjectCreate\|HasOwn\|ObjectHasOwn\|__proto__.*null\|prototype.*null" /workspace/node/lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js | head -30

# Look for specific prototype pollution defense patterns - focusing on the most relevant files
# First, let's understand freeze_intrinsics.js
head -60 /workspace/node/lib/internal/freeze_intrinsics.js

# Look at the key utility files for prototype pollution defense
# Check for safeHasOwnProperty and similar patterns
grep -rn "safeHasOwnProp\|SafeHasOwn\|ObjectHasOwn\|ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | grep -v "test_runner\|reporter" | head -40

grep -rn "ObjectHasOwn\|ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | grep -v "test_runner\|reporter" | grep -v "^.*:.*import\|^.*:.*require\|^.*://\|= {" | grep "ObjectHasOwn\b\|ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty" | grep -v "^\s*//" | wc -l

# Get the complete list of files with ObjectHasOwn or ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty
grep -rl "ObjectHasOwn\b\|ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | sort

# Let's check for safe merge/assign patterns in core utilities
# Look for Object.assign with null prototype or safe assign patterns
grep -rl "ObjectAssign\|safeAssign\|safeMerge\|Object\.assign" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | sort | head -30

# Look at key files: freeze_intrinsics.js, primordials.js, and util.js
# These are the core utility files for prototype pollution defense
cat /workspace/node/lib/internal/freeze_intrinsics.js | grep -n "function\|const\|let\|var" | head -50

# Let's look at the key symbols/functions defined in primordials.js related to prototype pollution
grep -n "ObjectCreate\|ObjectSetPrototypeOf\|ObjectFreeze\|ObjectHasOwn\|ObjectGetPrototypeOf" /workspace/node/lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js | head -30

# Check what functions/symbols are exported in the key utility files
grep -n "module.exports\|exports\." /workspace/node/lib/internal/util.js | head -30
grep -n "ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty\|hasOwnProperty\|__proto__.*null\|Object\.create(null)" /workspace/node/lib/internal/util.js | head -30

# Let's look at the main util.js and how it exports safe patterns
grep -n "ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty\|__proto__.*null\|Object\.create(null)" /workspace/node/lib/util.js | head -20

# Let's look at the key exported functions in internal/util.js related to prototype pollution defense
sed -n '730,760p' /workspace/node/lib/internal/util.js

# Let's see the full list of exports from internal/util.js to identify key symbols
sed -n '969,1050p' /workspace/node/lib/internal/util.js

# Look at primordials.js to understand what Object-related safe wrappers are exported
grep -n "^  Object\|^const Object\|^primordials\.Object" /workspace/node/lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js | head -40
tail -100 /workspace/node/lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js

# Find files with the safe merge/assign patterns - specifically using null-prototype objects
# for merging/assignment protection
grep -rn "{ __proto__: null.*\.\.\." /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | head -20

# Look for specific patterns that are used for safe merging
grep -rn "filterOwnProperties\|setOwnProperty\|kEmptyObject\|kEnumerableProperty" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | grep -v "^/workspace/node/lib/internal/util.js" | head -30

# Get the full list of files using kEmptyObject
grep -rl "kEmptyObject\|filterOwnProperties\|setOwnProperty" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | sort

# Also find files using SafeMap, SafeSet, SafeWeakMap, SafeWeakSet from primordials (null-prototype safe collections)
grep -rl "SafeMap\|SafeSet\|SafeWeakMap\|SafeWeakRef\|SafeWeakSet" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | sort | head -40

# Look at the SafeIterator and Safe* classes in primordials
grep -n "class Safe\|SafeMap\|SafeSet\|SafeWeakMap\|SafeWeakRef\|SafeIterator" /workspace/node/lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js | head -30

# Look at the specific functions in parse_args.js which handles safe property access
grep -n "hasOwn\|__proto__.*null\|Object\.create(null)\|filterOwn\|safeAssign" /workspace/node/lib/internal/util/parse_args/parse_args.js | head -20
grep -n "objectGetOwn\|ObjectHasOwn\|hasOwn\|__proto__.*null\|Object\.create" /workspace/node/lib/internal/util/parse_args/utils.js
grep -n "objectGetOwn\|ObjectHasOwn\|hasOwn" /workspace/node/lib/internal/util/parse_args/parse_args.js | head -20

# Get the full list of key files + check querystring.js which historically was vulnerable 
grep -n "__proto__.*null\|Object\.create(null)\|hasOwnProperty\|ObjectHasOwn\|ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty" /workspace/node/lib/querystring.js | head -20
grep -n "__proto__.*null\|Object\.create(null)\|hasOwnProperty\|ObjectHasOwn\|ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty" /workspace/node/lib/internal/url.js | head -20

# Look at querystring.js more closely since it parses user input and has prototype pollution history
grep -n "create(null)\|__proto__\|hasOwn" /workspace/node/lib/querystring.js
head -50 /workspace/node/lib/querystring.js
grep -n "create(null)\|__proto__\|hasOwn\|prototype" /workspace/node/lib/internal/querystring.js | head -20

# The key defense patterns in querystring.parse - let's check
grep -n "create(null)\|__proto__\|hasOwn" /workspace/node/lib/querystring.js
sed -n '310,330p' /workspace/node/lib/querystring.js

# What function is this on line 318?
grep -n "^function\|^const\|^module\." /workspace/node/lib/querystring.js | head -20

# Let's check source_map and http2 for Object.create(null) patterns
grep -n "create(null)\|__proto__.*null\|hasOwn" /workspace/node/lib/internal/source_map/source_map.js | head -10
grep -n "create(null)\|__proto__.*null\|hasOwn" /workspace/node/lib/internal/http2/compat.js | head -20

# Check specifically what the comment says about this in http2/compat.js
sed -n '315,325p' /workspace/node/lib/internal/http2/compat.js

# Check internal/modules/cjs/loader.js which is key for module loading
grep -n "hasOwn\|__proto__.*null\|Object\.create(null)" /workspace/node/lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js | head -20

# Look at key functions in primordials.js
grep -n "^primordials\.\|^function make\|^const make" /workspace/node/lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js | head -40

# Look at the makeSafe function
sed -n '360,410p' /workspace/node/lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js

# Let's look at the deepFreeze function in freeze_intrinsics.js
sed -n '377,540p' /workspace/node/lib/internal/freeze_intrinsics.js

# Check the complete list of unique JS files in lib/ that use prototype pollution defense patterns
# Combine all the patterns into a final sorted unique list
{
  grep -rl "Object\.create(null)\|{ __proto__: null" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js"
  grep -rl "ObjectHasOwn\b\|ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js"
  grep -rl "kEmptyObject\|filterOwnProperties\|setOwnProperty" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js"
  echo "/workspace/node/lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js"
  echo "/workspace/node/lib/internal/freeze_intrinsics.js"
} | sort -u | sed 's|/workspace/node/||'

# Check specific core utility symbols - the key functions and constants
# Let's get the key symbols from internal/util.js
grep -n "^function \|^const k\|^module.exports" /workspace/node/lib/internal/util.js | grep -v "//\|kCustomPromisify" | head -30

# Also check the internal/bootstrap/realm.js which handles module realm and prototype pollution
grep -n "hasOwn\|__proto__.*null\|prototype.*null\|ObjectCreate" /workspace/node/lib/internal/bootstrap/realm.js | head -20
sed -n '195,210p' /workspace/node/lib/internal/bootstrap/realm.js

# Check the bootstrap/node.js - the main bootstrap file
grep -n "hasOwn\|__proto__.*null\|Object\.create(null)\|freeze\|ObjectSetPrototypeOf" /workspace/node/lib/internal/bootstrap/node.js | head -20

# bootstrap/node.js needs to be added too
# Add it to our list check
grep -c "__proto__.*null\|ObjectHasOwn\|ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty" /workspace/node/lib/internal/bootstrap/node.js

# Also check crypto/diffiehellman.js which is in our list
grep -n "__proto__.*null\|hasOwn" /workspace/node/lib/internal/crypto/diffiehellman.js | head -10

# Also bootstrap/node.js should be in the list
# Let's add it to our final combined list
# Let me also check a few more specific files
grep -rl "__proto__.*null\|ObjectHasOwn\|ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty\|Object\.create(null)" /workspace/node/lib/internal/bootstrap/ --include="*.js" | sort | sed 's|/workspace/node/||'

# Let's get a more focused list - files that specifically use null-prototype objects
# for data storage (not just property descriptors) or hasOwnProperty for safe checks

# Files using Object.create(null) or {__proto__:null} for data objects (not just property descriptors)
grep -rl "= { __proto__: null }\|= { __proto__: null,\|Object\.create(null)" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | sort | sed 's|/workspace/node/||'

# Now let's build the final comprehensive answer
# The key key files with most focused prototype pollution defense:
# 1. Core primordials and freeze
# 2. Files with null-prototype objects for data
# 3. Files with hasOwnProperty safe checks

# Let me look at what the hasOwnProperty files are vs the null-prototype files
echo "--- Files with hasOwnProperty (but may not have null-proto objects): ---"
comm -23 \
  <(grep -rl "ObjectHasOwn\b\|ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | sort | sed 's|/workspace/node/||') \
  <(grep -rl "= { __proto__: null }\|= { __proto__: null,\|Object\.create(null)" /workspace/node/lib/ --include="*.js" | sort | sed 's|/workspace/node/||')

# Node.js Prototype Pollution Prevention in Core

## Your Task

Find all JavaScript source files in nodejs/node that defend against prototype pollution in core utilities: Object.create(null) usage, hasOwnProperty checks, and the safe merge/assign patterns used in core modules.

## Context

## Prototype Pollution Defense in nodejs/node Core

### 3 Main Defense Layers:

**1. Primordials System (`lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js`)**
- The central defense — captures all built-in JS methods before user code can tamper
- Key exports: `ObjectHasOwn`, `ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty`, `ObjectFreeze`, `ObjectSetPrototypeOf`, `ObjectCreate`
- `makeSafe()` creates null-prototype subclasses: `SafeMap`, `SafeSet`, `SafeWeakMap`, `SafeWeakSet`, `SafeWeakRef`
- `createSafeIterator()` for null-prototype iterators
- Ends with `ObjectSetPrototypeOf(primordials, null); ObjectFreeze(primordials)` to harden itself

**2. Intrinsics Freezing (`lib/internal/freeze_intrinsics.js`)**
- Adapted from Google Caja/SES — transitively deep-freezes all JS intrinsics at startup
- `deepFreeze()`, `enableDerivedOverride()`, `enableDerivedOverrides()`

**3. Core Utility Patterns (`lib/internal/util.js`)**
- `kEmptyObject = ObjectFreeze({ __proto__: null })` — used as safe default in 60+ files
- `filterOwnProperties(source, keys)` — copies only own properties using `ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty`
- `setOwnProperty(obj, key, value)` — uses `ObjectDefineProperty` to bypass prototype setters

### Critical Specific Usages:
- **`lib/querystring.js` `parse()`**: `{ __proto__: null }` for parsed result — prevents `__proto__`/`constructor` keys from polluting `Object.prototype`
- **`lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js`**: `Module._cache`, `Module._extensions`, `Module._pathCache` all use null prototypes
- **`lib/internal/http2/compat.js`**: HTTP/2 headers explicitly use `{ __proto__: null }` (unlike HTTP/1)
- **`lib/internal/util/parse_args/utils.js`**: `objectGetOwn()` — safe own-property accessor
- **`lib/internal/bootstrap/realm.js`**: `getOwn()` — safe property lookup for module realm

  "text": "The nodejs/node repository defends against prototype pollution throughout its core using three main patterns:\n\n## 1. Primordials System (lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js)\n\nThe central defense is the **primordials** mechanism. This file captures all built-in JS methods before any user code can tamper with them. Key anti-prototype-pollution exports:\n\n- **`ObjectCreate`**, **`ObjectFreeze`**, **`ObjectSetPrototypeOf`**, **`ObjectGetPrototypeOf`** — Safe wrappers for Object methods\n- **`ObjectHasOwn`** / **`ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty`** — Safe own-property checks that bypass inherited `hasOwnProperty` on the prototype chain\n- **`ObjectDefineProperty`** / **`ObjectDefineProperties`** — Safe property definition bypassing prototype setters\n- **`makeSafe(unsafe, safe)`** — Creates null-prototype subclasses of Map, Set, WeakMap, WeakSet, WeakRef, etc. via `ObjectSetPrototypeOf(safe.prototype, null)` + `ObjectFreeze(safe.prototype)` + `ObjectFreeze(safe)`, exported as **`SafeMap`**, **`SafeSet`**, **`SafeWeakMap`**, **`SafeWeakSet`**, **`SafeWeakRef`**, **`SafeFinalizationRegistry`**\n- **`createSafeIterator`** — Produces iterator classes with null prototypes\n- **`hardenRegExp`** — Strips prototype-accessible flags from RegExp to prevent RegExp pollution\n- **`uncurryThis`** / **`applyBind`** — Prevents prototype-chain lookups for `Function.prototype.call/apply`\n- At the end, `ObjectSetPrototypeOf(primordials, null)` and `ObjectFreeze(primordials)` harden the primordials object itself\n\n## 2. Intrinsics Freezing (lib/internal/freeze_intrinsics.js)\n\nAdapted from Google Caja/SES, this module deep-freezes all JS intrinsics at startup:\n- **`deepFreeze(root)`** — Transitively freezes all objects reachable from a root, preventing prototype chain extension\n- **`enableDerivedOverride`** / **`enableDerivedOverrides`** — Converts data properties to accessor properties to allow subclass override while keeping the frozen prototype tamper-proof\n- Uses `ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty` throughout to safely check descriptors\n\n## 3. Core Utility Safe Patterns (lib/internal/util.js)\n\n- **`kEmptyObject`** — `ObjectFreeze({ __proto__: null })` — a frozen null-prototype empty object used as a safe default for options parameters across the codebase\n- **`kEnumerableProperty`** — A null-prototype property descriptor template\n- **`filterOwnProperties(source, keys)`** — Copies only own properties using `ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty`, returning a `{ __proto__: null }` result object\n- **`setOwnProperty(obj, key, value)`** — Uses `ObjectDefineProperty` to set a property ignoring prototype chain setters\n\n## 4. Object.create(null) / {__proto__: null} for Data Objects\n\nThroughout the codebase, null-prototype objects are used for data dictionaries to prevent prototype pollution:\n- **`lib/querystring.js`** `parse()`: `const obj = { __proto__: null }` — critical for preventing `__proto__`, `constructor`, and `toString` keys from polluting Object.prototype when parsing user-supplied query strings\n- **`lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js`**: `Module._cache`, `Module._pathCache`, `Module._extensions`, `relativeResolveCache` all use `{ __proto__: null }` to prevent pollution via malicious module names\n- **`lib/internal/http2/compat.js`**: HTTP/2 headers initialized with `{ __proto__: null }` (explicitly noted in comment as intentional, unlike HTTP/1 which uses plain objects for Key anti-prototype-pollution exports:\n\n- **`ObjectCreate`**, **`ObjectFreeze`**, **`ObjectSetPrototypeOf`**, **`ObjectGetPrototypeOf`** — Safe wrappers for Object methods\n- **`ObjectHasOwn`** / **`ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty`** — Safe own-property checks that bypass inherited `hasOwnProperty` on the prototype chain\n- **`ObjectDefineProperty`** / **`ObjectDefineProperties`** — Safe property definition bypassing prototype setters\n- **`makeSafe(unsafe, safe)`** — Creates null-prototype subclasses of Map, Set, WeakMap, WeakSet, WeakRef, etc. via `ObjectSetPrototypeOf(safe.prototype, null)` + `ObjectFreeze(safe.prototype)` + `ObjectFreeze(safe)`, exported as **`SafeMap`**, **`SafeSet`**, **`SafeWeakMap`**, **`SafeWeakSet`**, **`SafeWeakRef`**, **`SafeFinalizationRegistry`**\n- **`createSafeIterator`** — Produces iterator classes with null prototypes\n- **`hardenRegExp`** — Strips prototype-accessible flags from RegExp to prevent RegExp pollution\n- **`uncurryThis`** / **`applyBind`** — Prevents prototype-chain lookups for `Function.prototype.call/apply`\n- At the end, `ObjectSetPrototypeOf(primordials, null)` and `ObjectFreeze(primordials)` harden the primordials object itself\n\n## 2. Intrinsics Freezing (lib/internal/freeze_intrinsics.js)\n\nAdapted from Google Caja/SES, this module deep-freezes all JS intrinsics at startup:\n- **`deepFreeze(root)`** — Transitively freezes all objects reachable from a root, preventing prototype chain extension\n- **`enableDerivedOverride`** / **`enableDerivedOverrides`** — Converts data properties to accessor properties to allow subclass override while keeping the frozen prototype tamper-proof\n- Uses `ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty` throughout to safely check descriptors\n\n## 3.
